user_query:
I’m triaging performance regressions in our C++ codebase and need a deterministic, ranked report plus stakeholder communications from the latest profiles. Using only the local inputs below, please:

Inputs (all under input/):
- input/perf_baseline.csv — baseline function-level metrics
- input/perf_current.csv — current function-level metrics
- input/code_metrics.csv — function code size/complexity
- input/component_owners.csv — file-to-owner mapping
- input/meeting_context.md — meeting info (title, date, attendees, agenda)

Deliverables (create/overwrite these paths):
1) out/prioritized_functions.csv — a ranked list of functions present in perf_current with the following columns:
   - function
   - file
   - owner (from component_owners.csv by exact file match; if not found, set to "Unassigned")
   - baseline_time_ms
   - current_time_ms
   - delta_time_ms (current_time_ms - baseline_time_ms; if function not in baseline, treat baseline_time_ms = 0)
   - delta_percent (if baseline_time_ms > 0 then 100 * delta_time_ms / baseline_time_ms; else 100 if current_time_ms > 0 else 0)
   - samples_current
   - llc_miss_rate_current
   - branch_miss_rate_current
   - size_bytes
   - cyclomatic
   - bottleneck_class (one of: Memory-bound, Branch-mispredict-bound, CPU-bound, Other), determined from current metrics by:
     • If llc_miss_rate_current ≥ 20 → Memory-bound
     • Else if branch_miss_rate_current ≥ 5 → Branch-mispredict-bound
     • Else if cpu_percent ≥ 80 → CPU-bound
     • Else → Other
   - inline_candidate (true/false), where true iff size_bytes ≤ 64 AND cyclomatic ≤ 5 AND current_time_ms ≥ 20
   - priority_score, computed exactly as:
       bottleneck_factor = {Memory-bound: 1.2, Branch-mispredict-bound: 1.1, CPU-bound: 1.0, Other: 0.8}
       inline_bonus = 1.1 if inline_candidate is true else 1.0
       reg = max(delta_time_ms, 0)
       priority_score = ((reg * 0.6) + (current_time_ms * 0.4)) * bottleneck_factor * inline_bonus
   - rank (1..N), sorted by priority_score descending; break ties by current_time_ms descending, then by function name ascending

2) out/summary_email.txt — a drafted email to perf@team summarizing the top 5 by rank. Include:
   - Subject line: "Perf regression triage: current vs baseline"
   - A brief intro sentence
   - A bulleted list of the top 5 entries in order with: rank, function, file, owner, bottleneck_class, priority_score (numeric), delta_time_ms, current_time_ms; append "[inline-candidate]" on the line if inline_candidate is true
   - For each bullet, add one short ask tailored by bottleneck_class:
     • Memory-bound → "Please investigate cache locality and data layout"
     • Branch-mispredict-bound → "Please examine branch patterns and reduce mispredictions"
     • CPU-bound → "Please consider algorithmic improvements or vectorization"
     • Other → "Please investigate CPU usage and potential algorithmic options"
   - A closing line asking owners to follow up in the next triage meeting

3) out/meeting_notes.md — meeting notes prepared for the upcoming triage. Include:
   - Title and attendees copied from input/meeting_context.md
   - Agenda copied from input/meeting_context.md
   - A short "Summary" section listing the top 3 functions with owner, bottleneck_class, and priority_score
   - An "Action items" section with 3 checkboxes (one per top 3), each showing owner and function plus a one-line action using the same class-specific phrasing as in the email; add "Due: Next sprint"

Additional requirements and assumptions:
- Read all the listed input files to extract the needed content. Do not assume any internet access.
- For joins, match on the exact "function" and "file" strings where relevant; if code_metrics or ownership data is missing for a function/file, leave size_bytes/cyclomatic blank and/or set owner to "Unassigned".
- All numeric calculations must follow the formulas above. Do not change thresholds or factors.
- The out/prioritized_functions.csv must include every function appearing in perf_current, even if delta is negative (regression weight will zero out negatives via max(…,0)).
- Overwrite any existing files at the specified output paths.

Please proceed and produce the three artifacts under the out/ directory. All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
